name: genesis

metadata.name: genesis
metadata.summary: Implementation of FreeSWITCH Event Socket protocol with asyncio

documentation:
# What is Genesis? Genesis is a python library designed to build applications (with asyncio) that work with freeswitch through ESL. [![Tests badge](https://github.com/Otoru/Genesis/actions/workflows/tests.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/Otoru/Genesis/actions/workflows/tests.yml) [![Build badge](https://github.com/Otoru/Genesis/actions/workflows/pypi.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/Otoru/Genesis/actions/workflows/pypi.yml) [![License badge](https://img.shields.io/github/license/otoru/Genesis.svg)](https://github.com/Otoru/Genesis/blob/master/LICENSE.md) [![Pypi Version badge](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/Genesis)](https://pypi.org/project/genesis/) [![Pypi python version badge](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/Genesis)](https://pypi.org/project/genesis/) [![Pypi wheel badge](https://img.shields.io/pypi/wheel/Genesis)](https://pypi.org/project/genesis/) ## What is FreeSwitch? FreeSWITCH is a free and open-source application server for real-time communication, WebRTC, telecommunications, video and Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP). Multiplatform, it runs on Linux, Windows, macOS and FreeBSD. It is used to build PBX systems, IVR services, videoconferencing with chat and screen sharing, wholesale least-cost routing, Session Border Controller (SBC) and embedded communication appliances. It has full support for encryption, ZRTP, DTLS, SIPS. It can act as a gateway between PSTN, SIP, WebRTC, and many other communication protocols. Its core library, libfreeswitch, can be embedded into other projects. It is licensed under the Mozilla Public License (MPL), a free software license. By [wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FreeSWITCH). ## What is ESL? ESL is a way to communicate with FreeSwitch. See more details [here](https://freeswitch.org/confluence/display/FREESWITCH/Event+Socket+Library). ## Why asyncio? Asynchronous programming is a type of parallel programming in which a unit of work is allowed to run separately from the primary application thread. When the work is complete, it notifies the main thread about completion or failure of the worker thread. There are numerous benefits to using it, such as improved application performance and enhanced responsiveness. We adopted this way of working, as integrating genesis with other applications is simpler, since you only need to deal with python's native asynchronous programming interface. ## Docs The project documentation is in [here](https://github.com/Otoru/Genesis/wiki). ## How to contribute? If you are thinking of contributing in any way to the project, you will be very welcome. Whether it's improving existing documentation, suggesting new features or running existing bugs, it's only by working together that the project will grow. Do not forget to see our [Contributing Guide][2] and our [Code of Conduct][3] to always be aligned with the ideas of the project. [2]: https://github.com/Otoru/Genesis/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md [3]: https://github.com/Otoru/Genesis/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md ## Contributors Will be welcome ❤️ ## Author | [
@Otoru](https://github.com/Otoru) | | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------: |

modules.name: genesis.exceptions
documentation:
Genesis exceptions
------------------

Grouping of all errors that can occur in genesis.

classes.name: genesis.exceptions.ConnectionError
documentation: exception created to group all connection errors.

classes.name: genesis.exceptions.ConnectionTimeoutError
documentation: Occurs when the connection does not occur within the established timeout.

classes.name: genesis.exceptions.SessionGoneAway
documentation: Occurs when the session has already received a hangup.

classes.name: genesis.exceptions.AuthenticationError
documentation: It happens when we have a problem during authentication.

classes.name: genesis.exceptions.UnconnectedError
documentation: It happens when we try to send dice to a server once we connect before.

modules.name: genesis.inbound
documentation:
Genesis inbound
---------------
ESL implementation used for incoming connections on freeswitch.

classes.name: genesis.inbound.Inbound

methods.name: genesis.inbound.Inbound.__aenter__
documentation: Interface used to implement a context manager.

methods.name: genesis.inbound.Inbound.__aexit__
documentation: Interface used to implement a context manager.

methods.name: genesis.inbound.Inbound.authenticate
documentation: Authenticates to the freeswitch server. Raises an exception on failure.

methods.name: genesis.inbound.Inbound.start
documentation: Initiates an authenticated connection to a freeswitch server.

Inbound class
-------------

Given a valid set of information, establish a connection to a freeswitch server.

Attributes:
- host: required
    IP address associated with the connection destination server.
- port: required
    Network port where ESL module is listening.
- password: required
    Password used for authentication on freeswitch.
- timeout: optional
    Maximum time we wait to initiate a connection.

functions.name: genesis.logger.get_log_level
documentation:
Get log level from environment variable or return default (INFO).

Valid values for LOG_LEVEL are:
 - TRACE
 - DEBUG
 - INFO
 - WARNING
 - ERROR
 - CRITICAL

Returns logging level constant.

functions.name: genesis.logger.setup_logger
documentation:
Configure a logger with rich handler and conventional formatting.

Args:
    name: The name for the logger instance

Returns:
    A configured logger instance

functions.name: genesis.cli.utils.complete_log_levels
documentation: Autocompletion for log levels.

modules.name: genesis.outbound
documentation:
Genesis outbound
----------------

ESL implementation used for outgoing connections on freeswitch.

classes.name: genesis.outbound.Session

methods.name: genesis.outbound.Session.__aenter__
documentation: Interface used to implement a context manager.

methods.name: genesis.outbound.Session.__aexit__
documentation: Interface used to implement a context manager.

methods.name: genesis.outbound.Session._awaitable_complete_command
documentation:
Create an event that will be set when a command completes.

Args:
    application: Name of the application to wait for completion

Returns:
    Event that will be set when command completes

methods.name: genesis.outbound.Session.sendmsg
documentation: Used to send commands from dialplan to session.

methods.name: genesis.outbound.Session.answer
documentation: Answer the call associated with the session.

methods.name: genesis.outbound.Session.park
documentation: Move session-associated call to park.

methods.name: genesis.outbound.Session.hangup
documentation: Hang up the call associated with the session.

methods.name: genesis.outbound.Session.playback
documentation: Requests the freeswitch to play an audio.

methods.name: genesis.outbound.Session.say
documentation: The say application will use the pre-recorded sound files to read or say things.

Session class
-------------

Abstracts a session established between the application and the freeswitch.

Attributes:
- reader: required
    StreamReader used to read incoming information.
- writer: required
    StreamWriter used to send information to freeswitch.

classes.name: genesis.outbound.Outbound

methods.name: genesis.outbound.Outbound.start
documentation: Start the application server.

methods.name: genesis.outbound.Outbound.stop
documentation: Terminate the application server.

methods.name: genesis.outbound.Outbound.handler
documentation: Method used to process new connections.

Outbound class
-------------

Given a valid set of information, start an ESL server that processes calls.

Attributes:
- host: required
    IP address the server should listen on.
- port: required
    Network port the server should listen on.
- handler: required
    Function that will take a session as an argument and will actually process the call.
- events: optional
    If true, ask freeswitch to send us all events associated with the session.
- linger: optional
    If true, asks that the events associated with the session come even after the call hangup.

classes.name: genesis.cli.exceptions.CLIExcpetion
documentation: Base exception for CLI

modules.name: genesis.consumer
documentation:
Consumer Module
---------------
Simple abstraction used to put some syntactic sugar into freeswitch event consumption.

functions.name: genesis.consumer.filtrate
documentation:
Method that allows to filter the events according to a set 'key', 'value'.

Parameters
----------
- key: required
    Key to be searched in the event.
- value: optional
    Value needed in the last key.
- regex: optional
    Tells whether 'value' is a regular expression.

classes.name: genesis.consumer.Consumer

methods.name: genesis.consumer.Consumer.handle
documentation:
Decorator that allows the registration of new handlers.

Parameters
----------
- event: required
    Name of the event to be parsed.

methods.name: genesis.consumer.Consumer.start
documentation: Method called to request the freeswitch to start sending us the appropriate events.

Consumer class
--------------
Abstraction used to create valid event consumers.

Attributes:
- host: required
    IP address associated with the connection destination server.
- port: required
    Network port where ESL module is listening.
- password: required
    Password used for authentication on freeswitch.
- timeout: optional
    Maximum time we wait to initiate a connection.

modules.name: genesis.cli
documentation:
CLI module for Genesis.
------------------------

This module contains the CLI commands for Genesis.

functions.name: genesis.cli.version
documentation: Show the version and exit.

functions.name: genesis.cli.callback

params.name: typer.Option("--version", help="Show the version and exit.", callback=version)

documentation:
Genesis - [blue]FreeSWITCH Event Socket protocol[/blue] implementation with [bold]asyncio[/bold].

Run yours freeswitch apps without any external dependencies.

ℹ️ Read more in the docs: [link]https://github.com/Otoru/Genesis/wiki[/link].

functions.name: genesis.cli.discover.get_app_name
documentation: Get the app name from the module.

functions.name: genesis.cli.discover.get_import_string
documentation: Get the import string for the given module path.

functions.name: genesis.cli.outbound.dev

params.name: typer.Option(help="The host to serve on.", envvar="ESL_APP_HOST")
typer.Option(help="The port to serve on.", envvar="ESL_APP_PORT")

typer.Option(
            help="Variable that contains the [bold]Outbound[/bold] app in the imported module or package.",
            envvar="ESL_APP_NAME",
        )

typer.Option(
            help="The log level to use.",
            envvar="ESL_LOG_LEVEL",
            show_default=True,
            case_sensitive=False,
            autocompletion=complete_log_levels,
        )

documentation:
Run a [bold]Outbound[/bold] genesis app in [yellow]development[/yellow] mode. 🧪

It automatically detects the Python module or package that needs to be imported based on the file or directory path passed.

It detects the [bold]Outbound[/bold] app object to use based on the app name passed.
By default it looks in the module or package for an object named [blue]app[/blue].
Otherwise, it uses the first [bold]Outbound[/bold] app found in the imported module or package.

functions.name: genesis.cli.outbound.run

params.name: typer.Option(help="The host to serve on.", envvar="ESL_APP_HOST")
typer.Option(help="The port to serve on.", envvar="ESL_APP_PORT")

typer.Option(
            help="Variable that contains the [bold]Outbound[/bold] app in the imported module or package.",
            envvar="ESL_APP_NAME",
        )

typer.Option(
            help="The log level to use.",
            envvar="ESL_LOG_LEVEL",
            show_default=True,
            case_sensitive=False,
            autocompletion=complete_log_levels,
        )

documentation:
Run a [bold]Outbound[/bold] genesis app in [green]production[/green] mode. 🚀

It automatically detects the Python module or package that needs to be imported based on the file or directory path passed.

It detects the [bold]Outbound[/bold] app object to use based on the app name passed.
By default it looks in the module or package for an object named [blue]app[/blue].
Otherwise, it uses the first [bold]Outbound[/bold] app found in the imported module or package.

Event handler

"
Event iterator

This class is responsible for iterating over the events from the queue.

Otherwise, it uses the first [bold]Consumer[/bold] app found in the imported module or package.

Run a [bold]Consumer[/bold] genesis app in [green]production[/green] mode. 🚀

It automatically detects the Python module or package that needs to be imported based on the file or directory path passed.

It detects the [bold]Consumer[/bold] app object to use based on the app name passed.

Otherwise, it uses the first [bold]Consumer[/bold] app found in the imported module or package.

modules.name: genesis.parser
documentation:
Genesis parse
-------------
It implements the intelligence necessary for us to transform freeswitch events into python primitive types.

modules.name: genesis.protocol
documentation:
Genesis Protocol
----------------

Here we will group what is common to the ESL client for inbound and outbound connections.

methods.name: genesis.protocol.Protocol.start
documentation: Initiates a connection to a freeswitch.

methods.name: genesis.protocol.Protocol.stop
documentation: Terminates connection to a freeswitch.

methods.name: genesis.protocol.Protocol.handler
documentation: Defines intelligence to treat received events.

methods.name: genesis.protocol.Protocol.consume
documentation: Arm all event processors.

methods.name: genesis.protocol.Protocol.on
documentation: Associate a handler with an event key.

methods.name: genesis.protocol.Protocol.remove
documentation: Removes the HANDLER from the list of handlers for the given event KEY name.

methods.name: genesis.protocol.Protocol.send
documentation: Method used to send commands to or freeswitch.
